title: ferray-strings: StringArray is completely separate from Array — no trait interop
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy 2.0's string operations (numpy.strings) work on standard ndarray with fixed-width string dtype (U or S). The strings are stored directly in the ndarray's data buffer. ferray-strings uses a completely separate StringArray<D> type backed by Vec<String>. This means: (1) you can't mix string arrays with numeric arrays in the same data structure, (2) no .npy I/O support for string arrays, (3) no broadcasting with numeric arrays, (4) no shared infrastructure for indexing, slicing, or reshaping. Consider whether StringArray could use Array<String, D> (would require making String implement Element or a separate trait) or if a wrapper that delegates to Array for shape operations is feasible.
